item.title: 使用 AWS CLI 管理 AWS S3 | Managing AWS S3 using AWS CLI
item.description:
您可以将 S3 用于对象存储、托管网站或重定向请求。

在本文中，我将向您展示在使用 AWS CLI 时如何在本地环境中轻松管理 S3 存储桶。

先决条件

  1. 已安装 AWS CLI - AWS 提供的有关如何安装 AWS CLI 的教程
  2. 配置 AWS CLI - 来自 AWS 的关于如何配置 AWS CLI 的教程

您可以将多个命令用于 S3 和 AWS CLI。 默认结构是：

 s3 <subcommand> [parameters]

我将向您展示一些您可能会在日常工作中发现有用的命令，当然还有更多。

创建存储桶
aws s3 mb s3://mybucket aws s3 mb s3://mybucket --region eu-north-1
删除存储桶
aws s3 rb s3://mybucket aws s3 rb s3://mybucket --force
部署

这将使用 .aws 凭证文件中的默认配置文件

aws s3 sync build/ s3://mybucket

如果要使用自定义配置文件进行部署，可以使用以下命令

aws s3 sync build/ s3://mybucket --profile mybucketapp
将本地文件复制到 Bucket
 aws s3 cp index.html s3://mybucket
下载文件
aws s3 cp s3://mybucket/index.html <path where you want to download the file to>
删除文件

从存储桶中删除文件

aws s3 rm s3://mybucket/index.html

从存储桶中删除所有文件

aws s3 rm s3://mybucket --recursive
删除存储桶

删除一个空的 Bucket。 删除 Bucket 及其所有对象

aws s3 rb s3://mybucket --force
将存储桶设置为网站
aws s3 website s3://mybucket/ --index-document index.html --error-document error.html

概括

希望您发现这些命令很有用。 你有其他使用的命令吗？ 请在评论中分享它们。



原文:

Amazon Simple Storage Service (Amazon S3) is an object storage service offering industry-leading scalability, data availability, security, and performance. S3 has 99.999999999% (11 9s) of data durability. You can use S3 for object storage, host a website or redirect request.

In this article I will show you how you easily from your local environment can manage your S3 bucket when using the AWS CLI.

Prerequisites

  1. AWS CLI installed - tutorial from AWS of how to install the AWS CLI
  2. Configure AWS CLI - tutorial from AWS of how to configure your AWS CLI

There are multiple commands you can use with S3 and the AWS CLI. The default structure is:

s3 <subcommand> [parameters]

I will show you some commands that you might find useful in your daily work and of course there are plenty more.

Create a Bucket 
aws s3 mb s3://mybucket
aws s3 mb s3://mybucket --region eu-north-1
Remove a Bucket 
aws s3 rb s3://mybucket
aws s3 rb s3://mybucket --force
Deploy

This will use the default profile in your .aws credentials file

aws s3 sync build/ s3://mybucket

If you want to deploy using a custom profile you can use the following command

aws s3 sync build/ s3://mybucket --profile mybucketapp
ls commands 
aws s3 ls
aws s3 ls s3://mybucket
aws s3 ls s3://mybucket --recursive
aws s3 ls s3://mybucket --recursive  --human-readable --summarize
Copy a local file to Bucket 
aws s3 cp index.html s3://mybucket
Download a file 
aws s3 cp s3://mybucket/index.html <path where you want to download the file to>
Delete file/s

Delete a file from Bucket

aws s3 rm s3://mybucket/index.html

Delete all files from a bucket

aws s3 rm s3://mybucket --recursive
Delete Bucket

Delete an empty Bucket. rb = remove bucket

Delete a Bucket and all it's objects

aws s3 rb s3://mybucket --force
Set bucket as a website 
aws s3 website s3://mybucket/ --index-document index.html --error-document error.html

Summary

Hope you found these commands useful. Do you have other commands you use? Please share them in the comments.
item.author: rashwanlazkani

item.title: 欢迎来到TestClub！🤜🤛 | Welcome to the TestClub!🤜🤛
item.description:
亚历克斯·普什卡列夫

发表于我一直在思考我的博客的真正目的是什么，我认为这是最好的。

我的职业生涯始于在一家没人知道的公司中自学成才的 Web 开发人员，然后我成为白俄罗斯和乌克兰最大的 IT 服务提供商的测试工程师，然后我成为测试自动化架构师，甚至是短期经理同时最终找到自己在最令人兴奋的公司之一的高级软件工程师。 我现在也在攻读计算机科学硕士学位。

我犯了相当多的学习和职业错误，并获得了很多有趣和有用（尽管有时很痛苦）的经历。

我相信我可以通过分享这些经验教训来帮助社区，这样您就不必费力地学习它们。

感兴趣的？ 那么请随时关注我。

历久弥新—— 一旦暂停，daddyasasha 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，daddyasasha 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发表，daddyasasha 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 dadyasasha 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。



原文:

Alex Pushkarev

Posted on Jul 18

👉Our mission is to demystify development so more testers know how to switch.
👉We also demystify testing so developers could write better software.

I have been thinking for a long time what is a true purpose of my blog and I think it's the best one.

I started my career as a self-taught web developer in a company nobody will ever know about, then I became a test engineer in a largest IT service provider in Belarus and Ukraine, then I became test automation architect and even a manager for a short while to finally find myself Senior Software Engineer in one of the most exciting companies out there. I am also pursuing masters degree in computer science now.

I made my fair share of learning and career mistakes and got quite a lot of interesting and useful (though sometimes painful) experiences.

I believe I can help the community by sharing those lessons learned so you don't have to learn them hard way.

Interested? Then feel free to follow me.

All-time-devlopment - Jul 15

item.title: AWS Cost Explorer - 成本异常检测报告识别出未经授权的 Amazon Sagemaker Canvas 用户 | AWS Cost Explorer - Cost Anomaly Detection Report identifed an unauthorized Amazon Sagemaker Canvas user
item.description:
这个月我收到了一个巨大的惊喜，一个与我的 AWS 根账户相关联的 7 月月底账单的预测为 586 美元。 作为最佳实践，我从不使用我的 AWS 根账户，总是使用 AWS IAM 账户登录来构建项目。

我最近没有使用过 Amazon Sagemaker 🙄，这引起了我对意外收费的担忧。

亚马逊成本管理器

通过导航到 Amazon Cost Explorer，AWS Cost Management 提供了成本摘要视图、右侧的评论以及概述 US-East-1（弗吉尼亚北部）地区每日使用量增加 270% 的图表。

我浏览了每个窗格以调查有关所产生成本的更多信息，例如区域、实例类型和关联账户。

实例类型

我检查了类型下是否有任何活动的 EC2 实例。 使用类型

在此窗格中，我可以确定 2022 年 7 月 11 日之后，有人在 7 月 11 日至 17 日期间创建了 Amazon Sagemaker Canvas 会话（以紫色表示）

关联账户

登录到您的 AWS 根账户并检查 Amazon Sagemaker 画布的任何活动会话。

降低成本 - 清理活动 AWS 资源

在 7 月 11 日的前一周，我清理了我的 AWS 账户中的活动资源，例如：

降低成本 - 创建成本异常检测报告

作为一项额外措施，我创建了一份成本异常报告，该报告可以通过电子邮件发送给我，以识别我的 AWS 账户中超过 15 美元阈值的任何可疑活动。 您可以从此链接创建成本异常检测报告

您还可以将成本异常检测报告下载为 csv 文件，以查看意外费用的详细信息：

降低成本 - 如果不使用则删除 - Amazon Sagemaker Canvas

导航到 Amazon Sagemaker Canvas 并单击Canvas ，然后单击Getting Started 。 我找到了一个未经授权的用户“michael-c”的活动会话，他在 7 月 13 日至 18 日期间在我的帐户上每天产生 40 美元的工作负载。 我按照说明修复并防止未经授权的用户访问此链接

我从 Amazon Sagemaker Canvas 中删除了该应用程序，然后删除了该用户。

从图中您可能会看到未经授权的用户“michael-c”。

我进一步探索了 AWS Cloud Watch 以确认用户“michael-c”在美国东部地区（弗吉尼亚北部）使用的 Amazon Sagemaker Canvas 的使用水平，以了解从 Canvas 实例向我收费的天数。

删除和阻止公共访问 - Amazon S3 存储桶

我想查看我的 Amazon S3 存储桶，看看我是否可以进一步减少每月的账单。 这是我发现的：

我清空了存储桶中的文件。

我将设置更新为Block Public S3 buckets 。

安全层 - AWS IAM

为了进一步保护我的 AWS 根账户，我还按照 AWS IAM 控制面板上的建议实施了多重身份验证 (MFA)。

启用 MFA 的最佳实践指南包括为 iPhone 或 Android 手机下载 Twilio Authy 并将其链接到您的移动设备。 您可以在 此处找到这些步骤。

最后步骤 - 更改密码

作为防止未经授权访问我的 AWS 账户的额外措施，我还重置了 AWS IAM 和 Root 用户账户的密码。

我希望你不必像我一样艰难地学习。 直到下一次，快乐的学习！ 😁 😁



原文:

I received a huge surprise this month, a forecast for a July month end bill of $586 linked to my AWS root account. As best practice I never use my AWS root account and always build projects with the AWS IAM account login.

The AWS Management Console provides a summary of the main AWS services used forecasted costs, in particular indicating that Amazon Sagemaker had and end of month $578.57 forecast and a cost increasing by over 300% in the current month. I have not used Amazon Sagemaker recently 🙄 and this raised concerns for my unexpected charges.

Amazon Cost Explorer

By navigating to Amazon Cost Explorer, AWS Cost Management provides a summary view of costs, commentary on the right hand side and also a graph outlining daily usage had increased by 270% in the region US-East-1 (N.Virginia).

I navigated each of the panes to investigate further information regarding the costs incurred such as Region, Instance Type and Linked Account.

Instance Type

I checked under Type to see if there were any active EC2 instances. There were no active instances after 11 July 2022.

Usage Type

In this pane, I could identify that after 11 July 2022, someone had created an Amazon Sagemaker Canvas session from 11-17 July (indicated in purple)

Linked Account

Login to your AWS root account and check any active sessions of Amazon Sagemaker canvas.

Reduce Costs - Clean up Active AWS resources

The previous week on the 11 July, I cleaned up my AWS account for active resources such as:

a) Deleting EC2 instances in the regions Sydney, Ohio and N.Virginia

b) Shutting down any active Cloud9 sessions

c) Deleting any active Cloud Formation stacks

d) Deleting any AWS Sagemaker processing jobs

e) Emptying S3 buckets for completed AWS Glue jobs

Reduce Costs - Create a Cost Anomaly Detection Report

As an extra measure I created a Cost Anomaly Report that could be emailed to me to identify any suspicious activity to my AWS account over a threshold of $15. You may create a Cost Anomaly Detection Report from this link

You may also download as a csv file the Cost Anomaly Detection Report to view the details of the surprise charges:

Reduce Costs - Delete if not in use - Amazon Sagemaker Canvas

Navigate to Amazon Sagemaker Canvas and click on Canvas and then click Getting Started. I located active sessions of an unauthorized user 'michael-c' who has been generating workloads $40 per day from 13-18 July on my account. I followed the instructions to remediate and prevent unauthorized user from this link

I deleted the app from Amazon Sagemaker Canvas and then deleted the user.

From the diagram you may see an unauthorized user 'michael-c'.

AWS Cloud Watch

I further explored AWS Cloud Watch to confirm the usage levels of Amazon Sagemaker Canvas used by user 'michael-c' in US East - region (N.Virginia) to understand how many daysI was being charged from the Canvas instance.

Cloud Watch was able to monitor the metrics of an AWS Service and provide further evidence for my investigation of the surprise charges.

Delete and Block Public Access - Amazon S3 bucket

I wanted to review my Amazon S3 buckets to see if I could further reduce my monthly bill. This is what I discovered:

a) User 'michael-c' had created an S3 bucket with my AWS account number and included a prefix with region US- East-1 (N.Virginia)

I emptied the files within the bucket.

b) This S3 bucket was Public and not private. I updated the settings to Block Public S3 buckets.

Security Layers - AWS IAM

To further secure my AWS Root account I also implemented Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) as recommended on the AWS IAM dashboard.

Best practice guidelines to enable MFA include downloading Twilio Authy for an iPhone or Android phone and link it to your mobile device. You may find the steps here.

MFA provides an additional layer of security to identify the user during the login process of your AWS account.

Final Steps - Change passwords

As an extra measure to prevent unauthorized access to my AWS account, I also reset the passwords to the AWS IAM and Root user accounts.

I hope you won't have to learn the hard way like me. Until next time, happy learning! 😁
item.author: abc_wendsss

item.title: 来自 MaibornWolff 在 AWS EKS 上的分布式 SQL 的 IoT 基准测试 | IoT benchmark on Distributed SQL from MaibornWolff on AWS EKS
item.description:
我通常不是基准测试的忠实拥护者，但由于多种原因，这个很有趣：

  • 它由咨询公司而非供应商发布
  • 它重现了一个现代用例（从传感器获取物联网），其中 SQL 和 NoSQL 替代方案都有效
  • 他们针对每个数据库进行了优化，因此它还记录了该工作负载的最佳配置。 我为 PostgreSQL 和 YugabyteDB 的优化做出了贡献

基准在这里：
这篇博文展示了如何在 AWS Kubernetes 上运行它

我在 4 个节点上启动了一个具有 32 个 vCPU 和 64GB 的 EKS 集群：

 eksctl create cluster --name yb-demo3 --version 1.21 \ --region eu-west-1 --zones eu-west-1a,eu-west-1b,eu-west-1c \ --nodegroup-name standard-workers --node-type c5.2xlarge \ --nodes 4 --nodes-min 0 --nodes-max 4 --managed

这比使用我的 MaibornWolff 在不同数据库中发布结果的集群要小，但这不是问题。 我知道 YugabyteDB 的可扩展性，并且更喜欢用户去独立的测试结果。

我转到 MaibornWolff 项目：

 git clone https://github.com/MaibornWolff/database-performance-comparison.git cd database-performance-comparison

我安装了资源有限的 YugabyteDB 以适应我的集群（最初的 CPU 声明是 2 用于主服务器，7 用于 tserver）但是，我再次关注的不是吞吐量，只是检查所有配置是否最佳。

 helm install yugabyte yugabytedb/yugabyte -f dbinstall/yugabyte-values.yaml --version 2.15.0 \ --set storage.master.storageClass = gp2,storage.tserver.storageClass = gp2 \ --set resource.master.requests.cpu = 0.5,resource.tserver.requests.cpu = 4 \ --set gflags.master.enable_automatic_tablet_splitting = false \ --set gflags.tserver.enable_automatic_tablet_splitting = false \ --version 2.15

请注意，我还禁用了平板电脑拆分，因为您不想从一台平板电脑开始并在基准测试期间启动自动拆分。 如果没有它并且默认情况下所有num_shards和num_tablets ，哈希分片表将被拆分为每个 cpu 一个 tablet（例如，3 个服务器中的 12 个，每个服务器有 4 个 vCPU）。 我可能会提交 PR 以从yugabyte-values.yaml禁用自动拆分

我验证所有已启动并显示控制台的 url：

 kubectl get all echo $( kubectl get svc --field-selector "metadata.name=yb-master-ui" -o jsonpath = 'http://{.items[0].status.loadBalancer.ingress[0].hostname}:{.items[0].spec.ports[?(@.name=="http-ui")].port}' )

我还检查了用于启动yb-master和yb-tserver ：

 time python run.py insert --target yugabyte_sql -w 1 -r 1 \ --clean --batch 1000 --primary-key sql \ --num-inserts 3125000
 time python run.py insert --target yugabyte_sql -w 1 -r 1 \ --clean --batch 1000 --primary-key sql \ --num-inserts 31250000

当它运行时，我会显示一些统计数据：

 kubectl exec - i yb - tserver - 0 - c yb - tserver --\ / home / yugabyte / bin / ysqlsh - h yb - tserver - 0 . yb - tservers . default \ - d postgres << 'SQL' \ ! curl - s https : // raw . githubusercontent . com / FranckPachot / ybdemo / main / docker / yb - lab / client / ybwr . sql > ybwr . sql \ i ybwr . sql SQL


这对于理解性能很重要。 我们这里没有读取，只有写入：插入到 DocDB LSM-Tree。 这是因为连接定义了yb_enable_upsert_mode=on ：我们不检查现有行。 请不要盲目地为任何程序设置。 在这里，因为我用序列插入，所以我永远不会有重复，所以不需要检查主键上的冲突。 序列上有一些读取和更新，但由于缓存，开销可以忽略不计。 插入的结果显示了吞吐量，这里每秒从单个工作人员插入 38500 行：

 Workers Min Max Avg 1 38570 38570 38569

我检查所有行是否都存在：

 kubectl exec - it yb - tserver - 0 - c yb - tserver --\ / home / yugabyte / bin / ysqlsh - h yb - tserver - 0 . yb - tservers . default \ - d postgres - c " select count(*) from events " count ---------- 31250000 ( 1 row )

我将继续本系列的查询部分。 这是物联网中最重要的一个：可扩展的数据摄取。

清理

要清理实验室，您可以缩减到零节点（停止 EC2 实例）、卸载基准部署（再次调整它）、卸载 YugabyteDB（并删除持久卷）、删除 EKS 使用的 CloudFormation 堆栈，以及终止 Kubernetes 集群

# scale down to stop nodes eksctl scale nodegroup --cluster = yb-demo3 --nodes = 0 --name = standard-workers # deinstall the benchmark helm delete dbtest # deinstall yugabyte kubectl delete pvc --all helm delete yugabyte # cleanup CloudFormation stacks aws cloudformation delete-stack --stack-name eksctl-yb-demo3 aws cloudformation delete-stack --stack-name eksctl-yb-demo3-cluster aws cloudformation delete-stack --stack-name eksctl-yb-demo3-nodegroup-standard-workers eksctl delete cluster --region = eu-west-1 --name = yb-demo3

基准测试结果的结果，它们在
请注意，您可以使用它在托管数据库上进行测试，只需更改config.yaml中的连接字符串（如 Aurora）或您自己部署的 YugabyteDB 或其他。 调用是批处理的，您可以运行许多工作人员。



原文:

I am usually not a big fan of benchmarks, but this one is interesting for multiple reasons:

  • it is published by a consulting company and not by a vendor
  • it reproduces a modern use-case (IoT ingest from sensors) where both SQL and NoSQL alternatives are valid
  • they optimize for each database, so that it also documents the best configuration for this workload. I've contributed to optimize for PostgreSQL and for YugabyteDB

The benchmark is here:
https://github.com/MaibornWolff/database-performance-comparison
and this blog post shows how to run it on AWS Kubernetes

I start an EKS cluster with 32 vCPU and 64GB across 4 nodes:

eksctl create cluster --name yb-demo3 --version 1.21 \
--region eu-west-1 --zones eu-west-1a,eu-west-1b,eu-west-1c \
--nodegroup-name standard-workers --node-type c5.2xlarge \
--nodes 4 --nodes-min 0 --nodes-max 4 --managed

This is smaller than the cluster used my MaibornWolff to publish their results among different databases, but that's not a problem. I know the scalability of YugabyteDB and prefer that user go to an independent test results.

I move to the MaibornWolff project:

git clone https://github.com/MaibornWolff/database-performance-comparison.git
cd database-performance-comparison

I install YugabyteDB with limited resources to fit my cluster (the original CPU declaration is 2 for the masters and 7 for the tservers) but, again, I'm not looking at the throughput, just checking that all configuration is optimal.

helm install yugabyte yugabytedb/yugabyte -f dbinstall/yugabyte-values.yaml --version 2.15.0 \
--set storage.master.storageClass=gp2,storage.tserver.storageClass=gp2 \
--set resource.master.requests.cpu=0.5,resource.tserver.requests.cpu=4 \
--set gflags.master.enable_automatic_tablet_splitting=false \
--set gflags.tserver.enable_automatic_tablet_splitting=false \
--version 2.15

Note that I also disable tablet splitting, as you don't want to start with one tablet and have autosplit kick-in in during a benchmark. Without it and with all num_shards and num_tablets by default, the hash sharded table will be split into one tablet per cpu (for example 12 in 3 servers with 4 vCPUs per server). I'll probably submit a PR to get auto-split disabled from the yugabyte-values.yaml

I verify that all started and display the url of the console:

kubectl get all
echo $(kubectl get svc --field-selector "metadata.name=yb-master-ui" -o jsonpath='http://{.items[0].status.loadBalancer.ingress[0].hostname}:{.items[0].spec.ports[?(@.name=="http-ui")].port}')

I also check the flags used to start the yb-master and yb-tserver:

time python run.py insert --target yugabyte_sql -w 1 -r 1 \ --clean --batch 1000 --primary-key sql \ --num-inserts 3125000

time python run.py insert --target yugabyte_sql -w 1 -r 1 \ --clean --batch 1000 --primary-key sql \ --num-inserts 31250000

While this is running, I display some statistics:

kubectl exec -i yb-tserver-0 -c yb-tserver --\ /home/yugabyte/bin/ysqlsh -h yb-tserver-0.yb-tservers.default \ -d postgres <<'SQL'
\! curl -s https://raw.githubusercontent.com/FranckPachot/ybdemo/main/docker/yb-lab/client/ybwr.sql > ybwr.sql
\i ybwr.sql
SQL


This is important to understand the performance. We have no reads here, only writes: insert into the DocDB LSM-Tree. This is because the connection defines yb_enable_upsert_mode=on: we don't check for existing rows. Please, do not set that blindly for any program. Here, because I insert with a sequence, I will never have duplicates, so no need to check conflicts on the primary key. There are some reads, and updates, on the sequence, but thanks to the caching the overhead is negligible. YugabyteDB combines the scalability of NoSQL (fast ingest) with all SQL features (sequence here).

The result of the insert shows the thoughput, here 38500 rows inserted per second from a single worker:

 Workers Min     Max     Avg
 1       38570   38570   38569

I check that all rows are there:

kubectl exec -it yb-tserver-0 -c yb-tserver --\ /home/yugabyte/bin/ysqlsh -h yb-tserver-0.yb-tservers.default \ -d postgres -c "
select count(*) from events
" count
---------- 31250000
(1 row)

I'll continue this series with the Query part. This one is about the most important in IoT: scalable data ingest.

cleanup

To cleanup the lab, you can scale down to zero nodes (to stop the EC2 instances), deinstall the benchmark deployment (to tun it again), deinstall YugabyteDB (and remove the persistent volumes), remove the CloudFormation stacks used by EKS, and terminate the Kubernetes cluster

# scale down to stop nodes
eksctl scale nodegroup --cluster=yb-demo3 --nodes=0 --name=standard-workers # deinstall the benchmark
helm delete dbtest # deinstall yugabyte
kubectl delete pvc --all
helm delete yugabyte # cleanup CloudFormation stacks
aws cloudformation delete-stack --stack-name eksctl-yb-demo3
aws cloudformation delete-stack --stack-name eksctl-yb-demo3-cluster
aws cloudformation delete-stack --stack-name eksctl-yb-demo3-nodegroup-standard-workers
eksctl delete cluster --region=eu-west-1 --name=yb-demo3

The results for the results of the benchmark, they are on
https://github.com/MaibornWolff/database-performance-comparison#insert-performance

Note that you can use it to test on managed databases, just by changing the connection string in config.yaml like Aurora, or your own deployment of YugabyteDB or other. The calls are batched, and you can run many workers.
